Open Source AI.title: OpenAI Reveals GPT-5.6 Sol Cybersecurity Model, Restricts Early Access - Infosecurity Magazine
Open Source AI.titleZh: OpenAI Reveals GPT-5.6 Sol Cybersecurity Model, Restricts Early Access - Infosecurity Magazine
Open Source AI.content: [Infosecurity Magazine Home](https://www.infosecurity-magazine.com/) » [News](https://www.infosecurity-magazine.com/news/) » [OpenAI Reveals GPT-5.6 Sol Cybersecurity Model, Restricts Early Access](https://www.infosecurity-magazine.com/news/openai-gpt-5-6-sol-limited-preview/) # OpenAI Reveals GPT-5.6 Sol Cybersecurity Model, Restricts Early Access [News](https://www.infosecurity-magazine.com/news/) ## Written by ### [Alessandro Mascellino](https://www.infosecurity-magazine.com/profile/alessandro-mascellino/) News Reporter * [Email Alessandro](mailto:alessandro.mascellino@protonmail.com?subject=Email from Infosecurity Magazine website) * [Follow @a\_mascellino](http://twitter.com/a_mascellino) OpenAI has previewed GPT-5.6 Sol, a new flagship model it describes as its "most capable model yet for cybersecurity." Access however is restricted to a small group of vetted partners at the request of the US government. [Announced](https://openai.com/index/previewing-gpt-5-6-sol/) on June 26, the Sol preview is the first release in OpenAI's GPT-5.6 series. The company said it briefed US government officials on the models beforehand and, at their request, allowed preview access with partners whose names it shared with the government. The series introduces a new naming scheme with three tiers: the flagship Sol,  a mid-range model called Terra and a cheaper, faster model named Luna. OpenAI said it plans broader availability within weeks, as it works with the government on a cyber executive order framework for future releases. ## **A Government-Gated Preview** The company framed the limited rollout as a short-term step, not a permanent arrangement. It said such a government-access process should not become the default, arguing that it keeps the best tools from defenders and enterprises who need them. Nevertheless, OpenAI called the approach the fastest route to a wider release. For now, the GPT-5.6 models are available through OpenAI's API and its Codex coding tool to selected partners. *[Read more on OpenAI's cyber push: OpenAI Expands Daybreak to Help Defenders Patch Flaws](https://www.infosecurity-magazine.com/news/openai-daybreak-gpt-5-5-cyber/)* OpenAI called Sol its most capable model yet for cybersecurity, citing gains in long-horizon work such as vulnerability research and exploitation. On the ExploitBench test, the company said Sol matched [Mythos Preview](https://www.infosecurity-magazine.com/news/anthropic-glasswing-expansion/) while using about a third of the output tokens. Still, OpenAI said the model does not cross the Cyber Critical threshold in its Preparedness Framework. In tests against Chromium and Firefox, it found bugs and exploit building blocks, but did not autonomously produce a full working exploit. ## **A Bigger Safeguard Stack** Sol is reportedly better at helping defenders find and fix flaws than at carrying out attacks end to end, OpenAI said, and it came with the company's toughest safeguards yet. These include refusals trained into the model and real-time classifiers that can pause and vet risky outputs. The company said it spent more than 700,000 GPU hours on automated red teaming to find universal jailbreaks, attacks that work across many prompts, backed by human expert testing. Sol is priced at $5 per million input tokens and $30 per million output, with cheaper Terra and Luna tiers plus a Cerebras launch planned for July. All the performance figures are OpenAI's own, from a preview that it said is still being tested. *Image credit: Novikov Aleksey / Shutterstock.com* ## You may also like 1. ### [OpenAI Expands Daybreak to Help Defenders Patch Flaws](https://www.infosecurity-magazine.com/news/openai-daybreak-gpt-5-5-cyber/) [News](https://www.infosecurity-magazine.com/news/) 2. ### [Trump Signs Order Inviting Voluntary Review of Frontier AI Models](https://www.infosecurity-magazine.com/news/trump-eo-voluntary-frontier-ai/) [News](https://www.infosecurity-magazine.com/news/) 3. ### [OpenAI Enhances Defensive...
Open Source AI.contentZh: [Infosecurity Magazine Home](https://www.infosecurity-magazine.com/) » [News](https://www.infosecurity-magazine.com/news/) » [OpenAI Reveals GPT-5.6 Sol Cybersecurity Model, Restricts Early Access](https://www.infosecurity-magazine.com/news/openai-gpt-5-6-sol-limited-preview/) # OpenAI Reveals GPT-5.6 Sol Cybersecurity Model, Restricts Early Access [News](https://www.infosecurity-magazine.com/news/) ## Written by ### [Alessandro Mascellino](https://www.infosecurity-magazine.com/profile/alessandro-mascellino/) News Reporter * [Email Alessandro](mailto:alessandro.mascellino@protonmail.com?subject=Email from Infosecurity Magazine website) * [Follow @a\_mascellino](http://twitter.com/a_mascellino) OpenAI has previewed GPT-5.6 Sol, a new flagship model it describes as its "most capable model yet for cybersecurity." Access however is restricted to a small group of vetted partners at the request of the US government. [Announced](https://openai.com/index/previewing-gpt-5-6-sol/) on June 26, the Sol preview is the first release in OpenAI's GPT-5.6 series. The company said it briefed US government officials on the models beforehand and, at their request, allowed preview access with partners whose names it shared with the government. The series introduces a new naming scheme with three tiers: the flagship Sol,  a mid-range model called Terra and a cheaper, faster model named Luna. OpenAI said it plans broader availability within weeks, as it works with the government on a cyber executive order framework for future releases. ## **A Government-Gated Preview** The company framed the limited rollout as a short-term step, not a permanent arrangement. It said such a government-access process should not become the default, arguing that it keeps the best tools from defenders and enterprises who need them. Nevertheless, OpenAI called the approach the fastest route to a wider release. For now, the GPT-5.6 models are available through OpenAI's API and its Codex coding tool to selected partners. *[Read more on OpenAI's cyber push: OpenAI Expands Daybreak to Help Defenders Patch Flaws](https://www.infosecurity-magazine.com/news/openai-daybreak-gpt-5-5-cyber/)* OpenAI called Sol its most capable model yet for cybersecurity, citing gains in long-horizon work such as vulnerability research and exploitation. On the ExploitBench test, the company said Sol matched [Mythos Preview](https://www.infosecurity-magazine.com/news/anthropic-glasswing-expansion/) while using about a third of the output tokens. Still, OpenAI said the model does not cross the Cyber Critical threshold in its Preparedness Framework. In tests against Chromium and Firefox, it found bugs and exploit building blocks, but did not autonomously produce a full working exploit. ## **A Bigger Safeguard Stack** Sol is reportedly better at helping defenders find and fix flaws than at carrying out attacks end to end, OpenAI said, and it came with the company's toughest safeguards yet. These include refusals trained into the model and real-time classifiers that can pause and vet risky outputs. The company said it spent more than 700,000 GPU hours on automated red teaming to find universal jailbreaks, attacks that work across many prompts, backed by human expert testing. Sol is priced at $5 per million input tokens and $30 per million output, with cheaper Terra and Luna tiers plus a Cerebras launch planned for July. All the performance figures are OpenAI's own, from a preview that it said is still being tested. *Image credit: Novikov Aleksey / Shutterstock.com* ## You may also like 1. ### [OpenAI Expands Daybreak to Help Defenders Patch Flaws](https://www.infosecurity-magazine.com/news/openai-daybreak-gpt-5-5-cyber/) [News](https://www.infosecurity-magazine.com/news/) 2. ### [Trump Signs Order Inviting Voluntary Review of Frontier AI Models](https://www.infosecurity-magazine.com/news/trump-eo-voluntary-frontier-ai/) [News](https://www.infosecurity-magazine.com/news/) 3. ### [OpenAI Enhances Defensive...

Infra & Chips.content: * [AI & Machine Learning](/digital-transformation/ai-machine-learning) * [Data Centers](/it-infrastructure/data-centers) * [Finance](/business-management/finance) * [Cloud](/digital-transformation/cloud) * [News](/latest-news) # Firmus expands to Indonesia through AI access deal with Nvidia The 360MW data center under construction in Batam will house up to 170,000 Nvidia accelerator chips. The facility is expected to be operational by the first quarter of 2027. [Gigi Onag,](/author/gigi-onag)Senior Editor, APAC,Light Reading June 29, 2026 3 Min Read (Source: Firmus Technologies) The Australian AI infrastructure firm Firmus Technologies has announced a major partnership with Nvidia to build a 360MW data center campus in Batam, Indonesia. Batam is an island located just off the coast of Singapore. Firmus and Singapore-based DayOne are now constructing the facility that is expected to be operational by the first quarter of 2027. Unlike Firmus' Australian projects that target hyperscaler customers (the largest cloud computing companies), the Batam facility will be a multi-tenant project designed for AI-native customers. The partnership between Firmus and Nvidia is eight years long, running through 2034. Through an economic alignment model that includes revenue sharing and credit support, Firmus can procure Nvidia infrastructure for AI-native, enterprise and ISV customers. Through the partnership, Firmus will sell Nvidia-powered cloud services. Nvidia will earn standard product revenue and a share of the cloud revenue on the supported capacity. Related:[Australia connectivity surge: Google-Telstra deal and SMAP's Tasmanian extension](/cable-technology/australia-connectivity-surge-google-telstra-deal-and-smap-s-tasmanian-extension) Firmus expects to receive between $25 billion and $30 billion from committed offtake agreements during the first six years of the partnership. The agreement covers the deployment of up to 170,000 Nvidia AI accelerators across the Grace-Blackwell, Vera-Rubin and Vera platforms through 2027 and 2028. This makes the Batam campus one of the largest AI infrastructure developments in the Asia-Pacific region. This agreement also significantly increases Firmus' deployed AI Factory capacity in the region. "AI-native companies need access to scalable, energy and cost-efficient compute infrastructure to compete globally. This partnership with Nvidia provides AI-natives with unprecedented access to the most advanced AI accelerators in the world, with the certainty, scale, and flexibility that best fits their high-growth trajectory," Firmus Co-CEO Tim Rosenfield said in a [statement](https://firmus.co/newsroom/firmus-to-build-170-000-gpu-ai-factor-y-campus-with-nvidia-for-global-ai-natives) on Monday. He added that the partnership will provide small AI firms a leg up in a competitive field. "We have worked to figure out how to close the gap between the cost benefits that the large guys have access to, which they do because they have great credit ratings, and the guys that are up and comers. This is actually a really material way to level the playing field a little bit to give the next a chance to compete with the big guys," Rosenfield said, as [quoted](https://www.reuters.com/world/asia-pacific/australias-firmus-technologies-strikes-ai-access-deal-with-nvidia-2026-06-28/) by Reuters. ##### Strategic significance This latest partnership between Firmus and Nvidia is one of the largest AI infrastructure deployments in Southeast Asia. It also establishes Firmus as a major player in the region's rapidly expanding AI ecosystem. The partnership also represents a significant expansion of Nvidia's DSX program, which partners with data center operators to deploy GPU infrastructure on a revenue-sharing basis rather than requiring upfront purchases – making advanced AI infrastructure more accessible to a broader range of customers. The Batam location is strategically close to Singapore, one of the most active financial and techno...

(NASDAQ: PLTR) today announced a strategic initiative with NVIDIA to deliver an intelligent engine for running NVIDIA AI and [Nemotron](https://cts.businesswire.com/ct/CT?id=smartlink&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.nvidia.com%2Fen-us%2Fai-data-science%2Ffoundation-models%2Fnemotron%2F&esheet=54561303&newsitemid=20260629390275&lan=en-US&anchor=Nemotron&index=1&md5=e3ef366cd054f8b8c826d2f84267d066) open models in sovereign environments, with a focus on United States government agencies and U.S. critical infrastructure, where open models are essential for national security, corporate sustainability, and industrial innovation. NVIDIA’s AI platform (compute, ecosystem, and open models) and Palantir’s critical infrastructure products (AIP, Ontology, Foundry, and Apollo) provide an intelligent engine for training and deploying open models alongside proprietary technology used for America’s most critical government agencies and commercial companies. Combined with Palantir’s domain-optimized harnesses, NVIDIA Nemotron open models deliver frontier capabilities for specialized workloads while enabling organizations to retain control of their data, intellectual property, and AI systems. Key capabilities of the offering include explicit data authorization, secure perimeter enforcement, architecturally-enforced customer-specific isolation, data portability, right to erasure, and full auditability. Using open source Nemotron models with Palantir AIP, Ontology, Foundry, and Apollo offers customers trust, accessibility, control and lower costs. **Customers will own self-improving models specific to their mission.** The intelligent engine collects and stores user telemetry and trace data, then uses that data to post-train and align the model to areas where AI can add the most value. With this feedback loop, the model continually improves at supporting specific operational tasks. Government agencies and critical U.S. companies can employ the NVIDIA-Palantir joint offering to retain sovereignty over their AI systems, giving them the ability to control and adapt open models, especially in regulated domains. This joint effort brings together: * **Deployment engineering**, enabling U.S. government agencies to access base and customized Nemotron models in classified, air-gapped, and other sensitive environments * **Context engineering**, enabling U.S. government agencies to optimize context, prompts,...

Jensen Huang, the CEO of computer chip giant Nvidia, was mobbed by onlookers as he hit the streets for the “zhajiangmian” noodles while visiting Beijing during U.S. President Donald Trump's May summit with Chinese leader Xi Jinping. But his celebrity status has not translated into success in selling Nvidia's advanced chips in China. Advertisement Article continues below this ad Controls imposed by Washington on exports of advanced technology due to national security concerns initially stalled sales of Nvidia's advanced H200 AI chips there. By the time Huang won a reprieve, with Trump agreeing to their sale, Beijing had switched to encouraging use of domestically designed chips made by local rivals led by Huawei. Infra & Chips.titleZh: The Unexpected Problem in AI Data Centers: Omen AI Proposes a New Solution - Zamin.uz
Infra & Chips.content: [Asosiy kontentga oʻtish](#main) * [Uzbekistan](/en/uzbekistan/) * [World](/en/world/) * [Society](/en/society/) * [Tech](/en/technology/) * [Sport](/en/sport/) * [Auto](/en/auto/) * [Culture](/en/culture/) * [Economy](/en/economy/) * [Ўзбекча](#) * [O'zbek](#) * [Русский](#) * [Türkçe](#) * [Қазақша](#) * [Français](#) * [Español](#) * [Deutsch](#) # The Unexpected Problem in AI Data Centers: Omen AI Proposes a New Solution ·13·[Technology](/en/technology/) The sharp increase in global demand for AI technologies is placing a massive burden on data centers. Efforts to maximize the use of powerful GPUs from giants like NVIDIA to increase computing power are leading to an unexpected technical issue: bacterial growth in liquid cooling systems. According to TechCrunch, the startup [Omen AI](/en/tags/omen-ai/), which has set out to solve this problem, has raised $31 million in [investment](/en/tags/investiciya/). This is reported by [Techcrunch.com](https://zamin.uz/index.php?do=go&url=aHR0cHM6Ly90ZWNoY3J1bmNoLmNvbS8yMDI2LzA2LzI5L29tZW4tYWlzLXBsYW4tdG8tb3B0aW1pemUtZGF0YS1jZW50ZXJzLWlzLWFsbC13ZXQv) reports. Modern data centers use a special liquid mixture to cool chips. Increasing the water content in the mixture to better absorb heat creates a favorable environment for rapid bacterial growth. This leads to clogged pipes and reduced system efficiency. Resolving the issue requires cleaning the entire system, which means shutting down servers for 5-6 hours. Such downtime can cost major tech companies millions of dollars. ## Real-time anti-bacterial monitoring The solution developed by Omen AI founder Zach Laberge is a miniature spectrometer capable of analyzing liquid composition in real time. This device monitors chemical changes and detects bacterial growth before the problem becomes serious. This allows [data center](/en/tags/data-markaz/) managers to avoid the risk of emergency system shutdowns. The capabilities of the Omen AI device are not limited to detecting biological contamination. The spectrometer can also detect particles of copper, chromium, or silicon in the liquid. This indicates that pumps are wearing out or seals are failing. Thus, the maintenance process is carried out on-site and quickly, without waiting for laboratory analyses. ## Young talent and a shift in the industry Interestingly, Omen AI founder Zach Laberge founded his first company at the age of 14 and raised $3 million in investment at that time. He even dropped out of school to pursue his ideas. Initially focused on creating sensors for construction equipment, Laberge encountered the cooling system problem in data centers while working with major clients like Caterpillar and pivoted his focus. The company's Series A investment round was led by Nava Ventures. Executives from giant companies such as Bridgestone, GM, and Johnson Controls also invested personally in the project. Experts believe that as AI infrastructure develops, the need for monitoring systems like Omen AI will only continue to grow. Even in the context of Uzbekistan, as digitalization and the construction of data storage centers accelerate, such innovative cooling and monitoring systems could play a crucial role in ensuring the stability of local IT infrastructure in the future. One consequence? Bacterial outbreaks. The liquid for liquid-cooled chips is a mixture of water and a substance that inhibits bacteria growth. To run the chips hotter, data center managers can change the mix to include more water, which absorbs heat better, but leads to nasty contamination that clogs the flow. To solve that, they flush the system, which can mean shutting down a rack for five or six hours at a potential cost of millions of dollars. [Omen AI](https://www.omen.ai/) has a solution: A tiny spectrometer that can monitor that fluid health in real time, spotting bacterial growth before it becomes a massive problem. “You’re not risking huge amounts of downtime because you have no insight into what’s going on chemically,” explains CEO and founder Zach Laberge. Laberge founded his first company in 2020 when he was 14, raising $3 million to install sensors on construction equipment and ultimately dropping out of high school. (His father and mother, a former Minister of Education for Ontario, were supportive of his plan to carve his own path.) After that startup shut down, Laberge started Omen in 2024, with the idea of focusing on fluid systems as they key to enabling construction machinery smart enough to know when it needed to be fixed. [Reviews ethics statement](https://www.cnet.com/home/how-we-test-everything/) * [Tech](/tech/) * [Computing](/tech/computing/) * [Laptops](/tech/computing/laptops/) # How to Choose the Right Dell AI Laptop for Your Workflow Copilot+ PCs bring dedicated AI hardware to everyday tasks. Here's what that means for battery life, productivity and choosing the right laptop. CNET BrandX for Dell [See full bio](/profiles/dell/) [CNET BrandX for Dell](/profiles/dell/) 4 min read Buying a laptop used to be fairly straightforward: You'd choose your screen size, decide how much power you need and compare prices. But if you're replacing an aging PC, you're probably seeing new terms like Copilot+ PC, NPU and local AI processing alongside the usual specs. Those aren’t just marketing terms. A dedicated AI processor can help improve battery life, power creative applications and save time throughout the workday. Dell's latest lineup includes options for everything from everyday productivity to demanding creative workflows. ### Dell AI Laptop Key Features * **Dedicated NPU:** Handles AI work efficiently while helping preserve battery life. * **Copilot Key:** One-touch access to Microsoft's AI assistant. * **InfinityEdge Displays:** More screen space with a smaller footprint on XPS laptops. * **Thermal Advanced Cooling:** Maintains performance during demanding work sessions. * **14-inch and 16-inch Options:** Portability versus a larger workspace. Here's what makes a Copilot+ PC different and which Dell AI laptop may be the best fit for your needs. ## What makes a laptop a Copilot+ PC? Copilot is Microsoft’s AI assistant, which comes built into newer Windows laptops. [Copilot+ PCs](https://www.cnet.com/tech/computing/what-is-a-copilot-plus-pc/) include a dedicated Neural Processing Unit (NPU) capable of delivering at least 40 TOPS (trillion operations per second) of AI performance. Unlike a CPU (Central Processing Unit) or GPU (Graphics Processing Unit) -- processors built for general computing and graphics -- an NPU is built specifically to handle AI-related tasks efficiently. A dedicated NPU is designed to handle AI workloads more efficiently than a traditional processor. That can help support features such as: * Live captions and real-time language translation * Background blur and automatic eye-contact correction framing during video calls * AI-assisted image creation and editing * Document summaries and writing assistance through Copilot * Improved battery efficiency during AI-enabled workloads **How Copilot+ improves your everyday workflows** The biggest benefit for AI laptop computer users may be a simple one: saving time. Copilot+ PCs can help automate small tasks throughout the day, including summarizing long documents, generating meeting notes and improving video-call quality. The dedicated, physical Copilot key on supported systems provides quick access to Microsoft's AI assistant without interrupting your workflow. Remote workers may notice smoother video conferencing and better battery life. Students can use AI tools to get organized and do research. Creators can accelerate editing and asset generation tasks without relying solely on cloud-based services. AI doesn’t make for a completely different computing experience, but it provides several small improvements that can really add up over the course of a workday. ## **Dell Inspiron Plus series: accessible AI productivity** ### **Dell 14 Plus: portable AI productivity** [Dell 14 Plus](https://cc.cnet.com/v1/otc/04Yxjj1rHOmYAxbnVDhvKNn?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.tkqlhce.com%2Fclick-3586864-17292208 "(opens in a new window)") [$940 at Dell](https://cc.cnet.com/v1/otc/04Yxjj1rHOmYAxbnVDhvKNn?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.tkqlhce.com%2Fclick-3586864-17292208 "(opens in a new window)") The [Dell 14 Plus](https://www.tkqlhce.com/cli...
